# Kinetiq ‚Äî full reference

> A React component library where every animation shares five calibrated springs. Copy the source. Own the code. Ship interfaces that feel machined.

Machine catalog: https://kinetiqui.vercel.app/registry-meta.json ¬∑ MCP server: https://kinetiqui.vercel.app/mcp

## Operating rules (AGENTS.md)

# Kinetiq ‚Äî Agent Rules

Operating rules for coding agents working with Kinetiq components. Follow
these and anything you compose stays on the system's motion vocabulary.

## The motion language: five calibrated springs

Every animation uses exactly one of five springs from `lib/motion.ts`. Never
hand-write stiffness/damping numbers ‚Äî reach for a named spring.

| Spring | stiffness / damping / mass | Use it for |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `flick` | 1100 / 55 / 0.7 | Press states, tick draws, focus. **Confirms.** |
| `snap` | 640 / 42 / 1.0 | Toggles, tab indicators, menus. One crisp overshoot. **Switches.** |
| `glide` | 300 / 34 / 1.0 | Layout shifts, reorders, morphs. **Moves.** |
| `drift` | 120 / 24 / 1.2 | Large surfaces, ambient motion, parallax settle. **Breathes.** |
| `recoil` | 520 / 24 / 1.0 | Toasts, stamps, landings. Two visible bounces. **Celebrates.** |

Say the verb the interaction performs ‚Äî confirm, switch, move, breathe,
celebrate ‚Äî and you have the spring.

## Tweens, not springs, for surface properties

Springs drive spatial change (position, scale, rotation). Opacity, color,
blur, and clip use the tween scale from `lib/motion.ts`:

- Durations: `blink 0.08` ¬∑ `fast 0.15` ¬∑ `base 0.24` ¬∑ `slow 0.40` ¬∑ `page 0.70`
- Easings: `enter [0.22,1,0.36,1]` ¬∑ `exit [0.5,0,0.75,0]` ¬∑ `move [0.65,0,0.35,1]` ¬∑ `linear`
- Enter offsets: `nudge 4` ¬∑ `step 8` ¬∑ `shift 16` (px). Scale-ins start at 0.96, never 0.

## Orchestration

- **600ms budget.** A choreographed sequence never exceeds 600ms total. Use
  `cascade(count)` for the stagger interval ‚Äî it tightens automatically as the
  count grows.
- **Exits are tweens at 0.6x the enter duration** with the `exit` easing, via
  `exitFor()`. Never spring an exit ‚Äî it reads as indecision.
- One spatial axis per element; stagger from the element the user touched.

## Reduced motion is a first-class state

Read `useMotionSafe()` ‚Äî never call `matchMedia` directly. When it returns
false, degrade, do not disable:

- Transform enters ‚Üí opacity fade at `fast`.
- Discrete springs ‚Üí instant position + a color tween.
- Direct manipulation (drag, sliders) ‚Üí keep 1:1 tracking; drop inertia and settle.
- Autoplay loops ‚Üí static (marquees become a wrapped grid).
- Scroll-linked ‚Üí final state, opacity only. Number rolls ‚Üí instant value + highlight.

## Tokens

Use shadcn semantic tokens only: `bg-background/card/popover/primary/muted/accent/destructive`,
`text-*-foreground`, `border-border/input`, `ring`. For live-data accents
(readouts, traces, LIVE badges) use `var(--signal, var(--primary))`. Radii:
`rounded-1` (2px) ¬∑ `rounded-2` (6px) ¬∑ `rounded-3` (10px) ¬∑ `rounded-4` (16px).

## Accessibility

Every interactive component ships real WAI-ARIA roles, complete keyboard
support, and visible focus rings (`:focus-visible`, never removed). Silent
state changes announce through an sr-only live region.

## Installing

Components are distributed as source through a shadcn-compatible registry.

```sh
# One-time: register the namespace
npx shadcn@latest registry add @kinetiq=https://kinetiqui.vercel.app/r/{name}.json
# Then add by name
npx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/pressure-button
# Or zero-config by URL
npx shadcn@latest add https://kinetiqui.vercel.app/r/pressure-button.json
```

Installed components import shared helpers (`lib/motion`, `lib/utils`, the
`use-motion-safe` hook) via your project's aliases ‚Äî the CLI rewrites them on
install. Don't edit those shared files per-component; they're the shared
vocabulary.

## Motion system

Five calibrated springs ‚Äî every animation uses one of them:

- `springs.flick` ‚Äî stiffness 1100, damping 55, mass 0.7 (Œ∂ 0.99, settles ~120ms). Press states, tick draws, focus. Confirms.
- `springs.snap` ‚Äî stiffness 640, damping 42, mass 1 (Œ∂ 0.83, settles ~300ms). Toggles, tab indicators, menus ‚Äî one crisp overshoot. Switches.
- `springs.glide` ‚Äî stiffness 300, damping 34, mass 1 (Œ∂ 0.98, settles ~450ms). Layout shifts, reorders, morphs. Moves.
- `springs.drift` ‚Äî stiffness 120, damping 24, mass 1.2 (Œ∂ 1, settles ~800ms). Large surfaces, ambient drift, parallax settle. Breathes.
- `springs.recoil` ‚Äî stiffness 520, damping 24, mass 1 (Œ∂ 0.53, settles ~700ms). Toasts, stamps, landings ‚Äî two visible bounces. Celebrates.

Cascade: Stagger interval = clamp(0.6 / (count - 1), 0.02, 0.06)s so the whole choreography stays under a 600ms budget; dense lists tighten automatically.
Exits: Exits are always tweens at 0.6x the enter duration with the exit easing ‚Äî springs on exit read as indecision.
Reduced motion: prefers-reduced-motion is a first-class state. Transform enters become opacity fades; discrete springs snap to position with a color tween; direct manipulation keeps 1:1 tracking but drops inertia; autoplay loops go static; scroll-linked renders its final frame; number rolls swap instantly. Every component reads useMotionSafe(), never matchMedia directly.

## Catalog

### Pressure Button (KQ-001) ‚Äî `pressure-button`
A button that pushes back.

A button that pushes back ‚Äî press squash on flick, spring rebound on snap, and a hold-to-confirm gauge ring for destructive actions.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/pressure-button`

Props:
- `variant`: "solid" | "outline" | "ghost" | "danger" (default "solid") ‚Äî Visual style. Danger pairs naturally with holdToConfirm.
- `size`: "sm" | "md" | "lg" (default "md") ‚Äî Control height and typography scale.
- `holdToConfirm`: number ‚Äî Milliseconds the button must be held before confirming. Adds a gauge ring; Escape or early release cancels.
- `onConfirm`: () => void ‚Äî Fires when a hold completes. Hold buttons never fire onClick.

Notes:
- Space and Enter drive the same physics as the pointer ‚Äî hold either to confirm.
- Under reduced motion the squash is replaced by a shade change; the gauge ring still fills because progress is feedback, not flourish.

### Trace Input (KQ-002) ‚Äî `trace-input`
Focus draws its own boundary.

Focus draws its own boundary ‚Äî a stroke traces the field's perimeter, the label glides to an overline, and errors annotate themselves with a dimension line.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/trace-input`

Props:
- `label`: string ‚Äî Field label ‚Äî rests as placeholder, floats to overline when focused or filled.
- `description / error`: string ‚Äî Helper and error lines below the field, chained into aria-describedby. Errors nudge the field and set aria-invalid.
- `prefix / suffix`: ReactNode ‚Äî Leading and trailing slots inside the field.
- `‚Ä¶input props`: Omit<InputProps, "prefix"> ‚Äî Standard input passthrough; controlled and uncontrolled both work.

Notes:
- The perimeter trace doubles as the focus indicator ‚Äî it is never removed, only accelerated.
- Under reduced motion the stroke appears instantly and the error nudge is skipped; the message still announces via aria-invalid.

### Breaker Switch (KQ-003) ‚Äî `breaker-switch`
A toggle with mechanical conviction.

A toggle with mechanical conviction ‚Äî the thumb stretches in anticipation while held, throws on snap, and squashes against the track end as the rail recoils.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/breaker-switch`

Props:
- `checked / defaultChecked / onCheckedChange`: boolean / boolean / (checked) => void ‚Äî Controlled or uncontrolled state.
- `size`: "sm" | "md" | "lg" (default "md") ‚Äî Fixed geometry ‚Äî travel is derived, nothing is measured.
- `onLabel / offLabel`: string ‚Äî Tiny uppercase text inside the track ends.
- `label`: ReactNode ‚Äî Adjacent clickable label, associated automatically.
- `name / value`: string ‚Äî Renders a hidden input that submits while checked.

Notes:
- A native button with role="switch" ‚Äî Space and Enter throw it, and Space arms the anticipation stretch.
- Under reduced motion the thumb repositions instantly; only the color crossfade remains.

### Checkbox (KQ-004) ‚Äî `checkbox`
The tick is drawn, not shown.

The tick is drawn, not shown ‚Äî a pathLength stroke on flick, an indeterminate dash that slides in, and group select-alls that cascade down the list.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/checkbox`

Props:
- `checked / defaultChecked`: boolean ‚Äî Controlled or uncontrolled checked state.
- `indeterminate`: boolean (default false) ‚Äî Mixed state ‚Äî dash visual plus native el.indeterminate.
- `onCheckedChange`: (checked: boolean) => void ‚Äî Fires with the next checked state.
- `label / description`: ReactNode ‚Äî Visible label (required) and muted secondary line.
- `CheckboxGroup`: items, value, onValueChange, selectAll, legend‚Ä¶ ‚Äî Fieldset-based group with select-all parent (checked / unchecked / indeterminate) and cascading tick draws.

Notes:
- Native hidden inputs do the accessibility work; the drawn box is decorative.
- Under reduced motion ticks appear instantly and select-all cascades lose their stagger.

### Caliper Slider (KQ-005) ‚Äî `caliper-slider`
Reads like a vernier, moves like grease.

Reads like a vernier, moves like grease ‚Äî 1:1 drag with a snap settle, ticks that lean toward the jaw, and a live measured-span dimension line in range mode.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/caliper-slider`

Props:
- `min / max / step`: number (default 0 / 100 / 1) ‚Äî Bounds and snap grid (float-precision cleaned).
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange`: number | [number, number] ‚Äî Single or range; live stepped values during drag, deduped.
- `range`: boolean (default false) ‚Äî Two facing caliper jaws with a live span dimension line.
- `marks`: number[] | boolean (default true) ‚Äî Every step when ‚â§ 40 steps, else quarters; or pinned positions.
- `format / readout`: (v) => string / "float" | "end" | "none" ‚Äî Value formatting and where the mono readout renders.

Notes:
- Full APG slider keyboard set per thumb; range thumbs cross-clamp their ARIA bounds.
- Under reduced motion tracking stays 1:1 ‚Äî only the tick lean and the settle spring are removed.

### Gantry Tabs (KQ-006) ‚Äî `gantry-tabs`
The indicator rides a rail.

The indicator rides a rail ‚Äî its leading edge glides ahead while the trailing edge lags a beat, stretching toward the destination before contracting to fit.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/gantry-tabs`

Props:
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange`: string / string / (value) => void ‚Äî Controlled or uncontrolled active tab.
- `variant`: "underline" | "segmented" | "enclosed" (default "underline") ‚Äî Indicator style.
- `orientation`: "horizontal" | "vertical" (default "horizontal") ‚Äî Layout and arrow-key axis.
- `activationMode`: "automatic" | "manual" (default "automatic") ‚Äî Activate on focus, or on Enter/Space.
- `keepMounted`: boolean (default false) ‚Äî Keep inactive panels mounted but hidden.

Notes:
- Full APG tabs keyboard support with wrapping arrows; disabled triggers are skipped.
- Under reduced motion the indicator teleports and panels crossfade without the lift.

### Drawer Accordion (KQ-007) ‚Äî `drawer-accordion`
Opens like a specimen cabinet.

Opens like a specimen cabinet ‚Äî height glides while the inner content lags a beat behind, like paper settling in a pulled drawer.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/drawer-accordion`

Props:
- `type`: "single" | "multiple" (default "single") ‚Äî Open one drawer at a time, or many.
- `value / defaultValue`: string | string[] ‚Äî Controlled or uncontrolled open value(s).
- `onValueChange`: (value) => void ‚Äî Fires with the next open value(s).
- `collapsible`: boolean (default true) ‚Äî Single mode: allow closing the open item.
- `Item.value / Item.disabled`: string / boolean ‚Äî Unique drawer id; disabled items are skipped in arrow navigation.
- `Trigger.icon`: ReactNode ‚Äî Leading icon slot before the header label.

Notes:
- Full APG accordion keyboard support: Arrow keys move between headers, Home and End jump.
- Under reduced motion drawers open instantly and content crossfades ‚Äî no lag, no sibling nudge.

### Callout (KQ-008) ‚Äî `callout`
A label with a leader line.

A tooltip with a leader line ‚Äî the label pops from the anchor while a schematic elbow line draws itself to the card.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/callout`

Props:
- `content`: ReactNode ‚Äî Label content. Never put interactive elements here.
- `side / align`: "top" | "right" | "bottom" | "left" / "start" | "center" | "end" (default "top" / "center") ‚Äî Anchor side and cross-axis alignment.
- `delay`: number (default 150) ‚Äî Hover open delay in ms. Keyboard focus opens instantly.
- `followCursor`: boolean (default false) ‚Äî Label trails the pointer with drift-damped lag; leader line omitted.
- `disabled`: boolean (default false) ‚Äî Never opens.

Notes:
- Opens on hover and on keyboard focus; Escape dismisses.
- Under reduced motion the card fades in place ‚Äî no pop, no leader draw, and followCursor pins to the anchor.

### Morph Dialog (KQ-009) ‚Äî `morph-dialog`
The trigger becomes the dialog.

The trigger becomes the dialog ‚Äî a shared-element morph glides the card you clicked into the panel it opens, and reverses on close.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/morph-dialog`

Props:
- `open / defaultOpen / onOpenChange`: boolean / boolean / (open) => void ‚Äî Controlled or uncontrolled open state.
- `size`: "sm" | "md" | "lg" (default "md") ‚Äî Panel width preset; ignored by the sheet variant.
- `sheet`: boolean (default false) ‚Äî Bottom-sheet variant ‚Äî full-width, pinned to the bottom edge.
- `dismissible`: boolean (default true) ‚Äî Escape, backdrop click, and the built-in close button.
- `portal`: boolean (default true) ‚Äî Portal to body, or contain the overlay in a relative parent.
- `Content.title / Content.description`: string ‚Äî Wired to aria-labelledby and aria-describedby; title renders as h2.

Notes:
- Focus is trapped while open and restored to the trigger on close; body scroll locks in portal mode.
- Under reduced motion the morph becomes a plain centered fade.

### Select (KQ-010) ‚Äî `select`
A menu that unfolds from where you asked.

A menu that unfolds from where you asked ‚Äî the panel scales from the trigger's edge, options cascade in, and a highlight bar glides between them.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/select`

Props:
- `items`: (SelectOption | SelectGroup)[] ‚Äî Flat options, labeled groups, or a mix; options take value, label, description, disabled.
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange`: string / string / (value) => void ‚Äî Controlled or uncontrolled selection.
- `searchable`: boolean (default false) ‚Äî Pins a filter input at the top of the panel.
- `placeholder / label / name`: string ‚Äî Empty-state text, wired visible label, and hidden form input.

Notes:
- Combobox APG throughout: aria-activedescendant highlight, wrapping arrows, typeahead, Escape restores focus.
- Under reduced motion everything fades ‚Äî no scale, no cascade, instant highlight bar.

### Telemetry Toast (KQ-011) ‚Äî `telemetry-toast`
Messages that queue like readings.

Messages that queue like readings ‚Äî recoil arrivals, a depth-receding stack, swipe dismissal with velocity projection, and timers that pause under your pointer.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/telemetry-toast`

Props:
- `ToastProvider.position`: "bottom-right" | "bottom-left" | "top-right" | "top-left" (default "bottom-right") ‚Äî Viewport corner for the stack.
- `ToastProvider.max`: number (default 3) ‚Äî Visible stack size; extras queue FIFO.
- `useToast()`: { toast(input) => id, dismiss(id) } ‚Äî Imperative API from anywhere under the provider.
- `ToastInput`: title, description?, variant?, duration?, action? ‚Äî Variants info | success | warn | danger; duration ‚â§ 0 persists; action renders a button.

Notes:
- Danger toasts announce as alerts; the region is focusable via an Alt+T hotkey chord and timers pause on hover or focus.
- Under reduced motion the stack flattens to a list with fade-only transitions ‚Äî the progress hairline still drains.

### Readout (KQ-012) ‚Äî `readout`
Numbers with momentum.

Numbers with momentum ‚Äî per-digit vertical rolls with real carry propagation, so 199 ‚Üí 200 visibly carries right to left.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/readout`

Props:
- `value`: number ‚Äî The numeric value to display.
- `format`: (value: number) => string (default toLocaleString) ‚Äî Formats the value; non-digit characters render as static cells.
- `size`: "sm" | "md" | "lg" | "xl" (default "md") ‚Äî Type scale.
- `rollOn`: "any" | "increase" (default "any") ‚Äî With "increase", decreases swap instantly ‚Äî right for latency-style metrics.
- `delta`: { value: string; direction: "up" | "down" } ‚Äî Change badge that flips up when the value moves.

Notes:
- Rolling digits are aria-hidden; a polite live region announces the settled value once per burst.
- Under reduced motion the value swaps instantly with a brief highlight pulse.

### Focus Text (KQ-013) ‚Äî `focus-text`
Copy that resolves under the lens.

Copy that resolves under the lens ‚Äî words sharpen from an 8px blur like a microscope focus-pull, cascaded under the 600ms budget.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/focus-text`

Props:
- `children`: string ‚Äî The copy to resolve.
- `as`: "span" | "div" | "p" | "h1"‚Äì"h4" (default "span") ‚Äî Rendered element.
- `by`: "word" | "char" (default "word") ‚Äî Split granularity; char mode never breaks mid-word.
- `trigger / once`: "inView" | "mount" / boolean (default "inView" / true) ‚Äî When the pull runs, and whether it re-blurs out of view.
- `stagger / startDelay`: number ‚Äî Seconds between units (defaults to the cascade budget) and lead-in delay.

Notes:
- Screen readers get a clean sr-only copy; the animated units are aria-hidden.
- Under reduced motion the block does a single fast fade ‚Äî text is always readable.

### Flapboard (KQ-014) ‚Äî `flapboard`
Split-flap departures for your data.

Split-flap departures for your data ‚Äî each cell flips through intermediate characters with a column cascade, like an airport board settling.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/flapboard`

Props:
- `value`: string ‚Äî Text to display, normalized against the charset.
- `chars`: string (default " A‚ÄìZ 0‚Äì9 ¬∑-") ‚Äî Charset scanned in flip order.
- `flipSpeed`: number (default 40) ‚Äî Milliseconds per flip step.
- `padTo / align`: number / "left" | "right" ‚Äî Minimum cell count and which side the value sits on.
- `size`: "sm" | "md" | "lg" (default "md") ‚Äî Fixed tile dimensions.

Notes:
- Cells are aria-hidden; a polite status region announces the settled value once.
- Under reduced motion the board crossfades to the new value ‚Äî no flipping.

### Scope Scrubber (KQ-015) ‚Äî `scope-scrubber`
Drag a value; watch its physics.

Drag a value; watch its physics ‚Äî a scrub input with a live oscilloscope trace behind the numeral, where the settle spring's overshoot draws itself.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/scope-scrubber`

Props:
- `min / max / step`: number ‚Äî Bounds (required) and release snap grid.
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange`: number / number / (value) => void ‚Äî Controlled or uncontrolled; fires live while scrubbing.
- `settle`: SpringName (default "snap") ‚Äî Which calibrated spring the value settles on ‚Äî its personality shows on the trace.
- `sensitivity`: number (default (max‚àímin)/200) ‚Äî Value change per pixel of drag.
- `label / unit / format / trace`: string / string / (v)=>string / boolean ‚Äî Labeling, suffix, numeral formatting, and the history trace toggle.

Notes:
- Enter or double-click converts the plate into a plain number input; Escape cancels.
- Under reduced motion scrubbing stays 1:1, release snaps instantly, and the trace hides.

### Metronome Loader (KQ-016) ‚Äî `metronome-loader`
Waiting, kept honest.

Waiting, kept honest ‚Äî a pendulum, a bearing cradle, or a radar sweep; give it a value and it morphs into a determinate arc gauge.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/metronome-loader`

Props:
- `variant`: "pendulum" | "bearing" | "sweep" (default "pendulum") ‚Äî Indeterminate style; ignored once value is set.
- `size`: "sm" | "md" | "lg" (default "md") ‚Äî 24 / 40 / 64 px.
- `value`: number ‚Äî 0‚Äì100; presence morphs the loader into a determinate arc gauge.
- `label / showLabel`: string / boolean ‚Äî Accessible name; optionally rendered visibly.

Notes:
- role="progressbar" throughout ‚Äî indeterminate announces Loading, determinate reports its value.
- Under reduced motion the loops freeze to a static glyph with a slow opacity pulse.

### Gyro Card (KQ-017) ‚Äî `gyro-card`
A card with a sense of balance.

A card with a sense of balance ‚Äî pointer tilt with real inertia that overshoots past level and rebalances when you leave, with layered parallax and tracking glare.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/gyro-card`

Props:
- `maxTilt`: number (default 8) ‚Äî Peak rotation toward the pointer, in degrees.
- `glare`: boolean (default true) ‚Äî Radial sheen that tracks the light angle.
- `GyroLayer.depth`: 0 | 1 | 2 ‚Äî Parallax depth for child slots ‚Äî pinned, ¬±4px, or ¬±8px counter-translation.

Notes:
- The motion is decorative: DOM order is untouched, and keyboard focus inside flattens the card to an energized ring state.
- Under reduced motion there is no tilt, parallax, or glare ‚Äî hover becomes a border emphasis.

### Magnet Dock (KQ-018) ‚Äî `magnet-dock`
Icons that feel the cursor coming.

Icons that feel the cursor coming ‚Äî a cosine distance field magnifies the dock, and drag-reorder parts neighbors magnetically before snapping into a slot.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/magnet-dock`

Props:
- `items`: DockItem[] ‚Äî { id, label, icon, active?, onSelect? } per icon.
- `orientation`: "horizontal" | "vertical" (default "horizontal") ‚Äî Dock axis ‚Äî pointer tracking, tooltips, and arrows follow.
- `magnify`: number (default 1.6) ‚Äî Peak scale under the pointer or keyboard focus.
- `reorderable / onReorder`: boolean / (order: string[]) => void ‚Äî Pointer drag plus Space-lift keyboard reordering, announced live.

Notes:
- role="toolbar" with roving tabindex; a focused icon magnifies without the pointer.
- Under reduced motion magnification is replaced by a 2px raise; reordering still works, 1:1.

### Conveyor List (KQ-019) ‚Äî `conveyor-list`
A queue that moves like a belt.

A queue that moves like a belt ‚Äî items drift in from the feed side, brake into their slot with a squash, and lift off the belt when removed.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/conveyor-list`

Props:
- `items / keyFor / renderItem`: T[] / (item) => string / (item) => ReactNode ‚Äî Generic queue contents with stable keys driving enter, exit, and reorder identity.
- `side`: "top" | "bottom" (default "top") ‚Äî The feed edge new items enter from.
- `maxVisible`: number (default 6) ‚Äî Rows shown before the oldest overflow into a +N more count.
- `announceItem`: (item: T) => string ‚Äî Screen-reader text per row for the live log region.

Notes:
- The list is a role="log" live region ‚Äî arrivals are announced politely.
- Under reduced motion rows crossfade in place and layout shifts are instant.

### Ticker Tape (KQ-020) ‚Äî `ticker-tape`
A marquee with friction.

A marquee with friction ‚Äî hover applies drag, grabbing tracks 1:1, and a fling carries real momentum that decays back to duty speed.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/ticker-tape`

Props:
- `speed`: number (default 60) ‚Äî Duty cruising speed in px/s.
- `direction`: "left" | "right" (default "left") ‚Äî Travel direction.
- `gap`: number (default 24) ‚Äî Gap between items and loop copies.
- `pauseOnHover`: boolean (default true) ‚Äî Ease to 30% duty speed while hovered.

Notes:
- A visible pause control surfaces on keyboard focus; duplicated sequences are aria-hidden.
- Under reduced motion the tape renders as a static wrapped grid showing each item once.

### Scan Reveal (KQ-021) ‚Äî `scan-reveal`
Sections develop as the scanner passes.

Sections develop as the scanner passes ‚Äî a scroll-linked scan line sweeps once, and content resolves beneath it with a coordinate readout riding the beam.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/scan-reveal`

Props:
- `once`: boolean (default true) ‚Äî Tear down the dual layers after the sweep completes.
- `direction`: "down" | "up" (default "down") ‚Äî Sweep direction.
- `containerRef`: RefObject<HTMLElement> ‚Äî Scrollable ancestor for scroll-linking inside overflow containers.

Notes:
- Interactive content lives only in the developed layer; the undeveloped copy is aria-hidden and inert.
- Under reduced motion content renders fully developed with a single fast fade.

### Radio Group (KQ-022) ‚Äî `radio-group`
Selection that travels, never teleports.

Selection that travels, never teleports ‚Äî one shared dot glides between rings while the receiving ring arms with a squash and rebounds as it lands.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/radio-group`

Props:
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange`: string / string / (value) => void ‚Äî Controlled or uncontrolled selection.
- `orientation`: "vertical" | "horizontal" (default "vertical") ‚Äî Layout and aria-orientation.
- `name / label`: string / ReactNode ‚Äî Hidden native radios for form posts; visible group label (or pass aria-label).
- `Item.value / Item.description`: string / ReactNode ‚Äî Item identity and a muted secondary line.

Notes:
- Full APG radio keyboard: arrows wrap and select, Space selects, Home and End jump.
- Under reduced motion the dot swaps instantly with only a border-color tween.

### Code Cells (KQ-023) ‚Äî `code-cells`
Six digits, dropped into place.

Six digits, dropped into place ‚Äî one hidden one-time-code input drives visual cells with digit drops, underline ticks, and a rejection nudge.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/code-cells`

Props:
- `length`: number (default 6) ‚Äî Cell count; also the hidden input's maxLength.
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange`: string / string / (code) => void ‚Äî Controlled or uncontrolled code, digits only.
- `onComplete`: (code: string) => void ‚Äî Fires once per fill; re-arms when the code shrinks.
- `error / errorMessage`: boolean / string ‚Äî Destructive borders plus a row nudge and a role=alert line.
- `groups`: number[] ‚Äî e.g. [3,3] renders a separator between digit groups.
- `ref`: Ref<HTMLInputElement> ‚Äî Reaches the hidden input ‚Äî focus() after clearing.

Notes:
- The single hidden input with autocomplete="one-time-code" is the accessibility-correct OTP pattern ‚Äî cells are decorative.
- Under reduced motion digits appear instantly and the nudge is skipped; the alert still announces.

### Action Relay (KQ-024) ‚Äî `action-relay`
A button that narrates the job.

A button that narrates the job ‚Äî state labels roll through a one-line mask with a blur crossfade while the width follows the new content.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/action-relay`

Props:
- `state / states`: string / Record<string, ReactNode> ‚Äî Controlled state key and the content per state ‚Äî nodes may embed icons or a small loader.
- `busyStates`: string[] ‚Äî States that report aria-busy and suppress interaction without stealing focus.
- `onSettled`: (state) => void ‚Äî Fires when the incoming roll lands ‚Äî chain done ‚Üí idle off it.
- `announcements`: Record<string, string> ‚Äî Screen-reader text per state; plain-text nodes announce themselves.
- `variant / size`: pressure-button scales (default "solid" / "md") ‚Äî Visual style, mirroring the button family.

Notes:
- Busy states keep focus (aria-disabled, not disabled) so the announcement chain is never interrupted.
- Under reduced motion labels crossfade and the width jumps; onSettled still fires.

### Status Seal (KQ-025) ‚Äî `status-seal`
Status, re-stamped on every change.

Status, re-stamped on every change ‚Äî variant swaps stamp in on recoil, a live pulse ring breathes at drift tempo, and counts carry-roll.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/status-seal`

Props:
- `variant`: "info" | "success" | "warn" | "danger" (default "info") ‚Äî Semantic tinting with token fallbacks for consumer apps.
- `children / icon`: ReactNode ‚Äî Label and optional leading node (defaults to a status dot).
- `count`: number ‚Äî Embeds a small Readout so numbers carry-roll without re-stamping.
- `live`: boolean (default false) ‚Äî Pulse ring behind the dot at drift tempo.

Notes:
- role="status" announces label and count once per change, debounced.
- Under reduced motion swaps are instant and the pulse ring holds static at mid-opacity.

### Slipstream (KQ-026) ‚Äî `slipstream`
A highlight that rides in your wake.

A highlight that rides in your wake ‚Äî one pill glides behind hovered or focused items with a trailing-edge stretch, in any direction.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/slipstream`

Props:
- `radius / inset`: number / number (default 8 / 0) ‚Äî Pill corner radius, and how far it tucks inside each item rect (negative bleeds out).
- `disabled`: boolean (default false) ‚Äî Parks the pill and ignores hover/focus.
- `SlipstreamItem`: div props ‚Äî Wrap each link or button; the wrapper registers its rect for the pill to target.

Notes:
- Keyboard focus drives the pill exactly like hover ‚Äî focusin on the group targets the focused item.
- Under reduced motion the pill teleports with a fast fade; no trailing stretch.

### Drawer (KQ-027) ‚Äî `drawer`
A side panel on rails.

A side panel on rails ‚Äî slides in on glide, drags toward its edge 1:1, and a projected release past 40% lets it leave with the momentum it already has.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/drawer`

Props:
- `open / defaultOpen / onOpenChange`: boolean / boolean / (open) => void ‚Äî Controlled or uncontrolled visibility.
- `side / size`: "left" | "right" / "sm" | "md" | "lg" (default "right" / "md") ‚Äî Edge and width preset (288 / 360 / 440).
- `dismissible / portal`: boolean (default true / true) ‚Äî Escape, backdrop, and edge-drag dismissal; portal locks body scroll.
- `Trigger / Content / Title / Description / Close`: compound parts ‚Äî Composable parts sharing one context.

Notes:
- The backdrop's opacity is bound to the drag ‚Äî dismissal fades exactly as far as you've pulled.
- Under reduced motion the panel fades; dragging stays 1:1 with instant resolution.

### Bottom Sheet (KQ-028) ‚Äî `bottom-sheet`
It snaps where your gesture was going.

It snaps where your gesture was going ‚Äî velocity-projected releases settle on the nearest snap point in the gesture's direction, with rubber-band overdrag.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/bottom-sheet`

Props:
- `snapPoints / initialSnap / onSnapChange`: number[] / number / (index) => void (default [0.4, 0.9] / 0) ‚Äî Resting heights as viewport fractions; every settle reports its index.
- `open / defaultOpen / onOpenChange`: boolean / boolean / (open) => void ‚Äî Controlled or uncontrolled visibility.
- `dismissible / portal`: boolean (default true / true) ‚Äî Projecting past the lowest snap dismisses; portal measures the visual viewport.
- `Handle`: compound part ‚Äî The grab pill is a real button ‚Äî arrows step snaps, Home/End jump, each step announces.

Notes:
- The backdrop opacity is bound to the sheet's travel, live during the drag.
- Under reduced motion it fades to its snap and releases resolve instantly; dragging stays 1:1.

### Cipher Text (KQ-029) ‚Äî `cipher-text`
Characters lock in from the noise.

Characters lock in from the noise ‚Äî glyphs cycle deterministically, then resolve left-to-right under the 600ms cascade budget with a signal pulse per lock.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/cipher-text`

Props:
- `children`: string ‚Äî Copy to resolve; split into per-character mono cells.
- `trigger`: "inView" | "mount" | "hover" (default "inView") ‚Äî Hover re-scrambles then resolves on every entry.
- `order`: "ltr" | "random" (default "ltr") ‚Äî Lock-in sequence; random is hash-shuffled, never Math.random.
- `charset / speed / as`: string / number / element (default A‚ÄìZ 0‚Äì9 ¬∑- / 30 / "span") ‚Äî Glyph pool, ms per scramble tick, rendered element.

Notes:
- Screen readers and no-JS visitors get the clean string; the scramble layer is aria-hidden.
- Under reduced motion the text renders plainly with a single fast fade.

### Ledger (KQ-030) ‚Äî `ledger`
Ten thousand rows, sixty frames.

Ten thousand rows, sixty frames ‚Äî a hand-rolled virtualized table where sorting FLIPs only the rows you can see and selection cascades down the window.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/ledger`

Props:
- `columns / rows / rowId`: LedgerColumn<T>[] / readonly T[] / (row) => string ‚Äî Column defs (width px | "1fr", align, sortable, cell, sortFn), the full data set, and stable row identity.
- `rowHeight / height / overscan`: number (default 40 / 320 / 6) ‚Äî Fixed row height, viewport height, and window padding.
- `sort / defaultSort / onSortChange`: LedgerSort | null ‚Äî Controlled or uncontrolled; headers cycle asc ‚Üí desc ‚Üí none.
- `selectable / selected / onSelectedChange`: boolean / ReadonlySet<string> / (next) => void ‚Äî Checkbox column; select-all targets every row, not just visible.
- `onVisibleRangeChange`: ({ from, to }) => void ‚Äî 1-based visible range, emitted from the rAF scroll flush.

Notes:
- Roughly twenty nodes render regardless of row count; sort animates at most thirty visible rows.
- Under reduced motion the chevron swaps instantly and rows jump ‚Äî no FLIP, no tick cascade.

### Zoetrope (KQ-031) ‚Äî `zoetrope`
Spin the drum; every frame finds its detent.

Spin the drum; every frame finds its detent ‚Äî panels ride the inside of a cylinder with drag inertia, wheel steps, and angular-distance dimming.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/zoetrope`

Props:
- `children`: ReactNode ‚Äî Each direct child becomes a drum face (capped at twelve).
- `index / defaultIndex / onIndexChange`: number / number / (index) => void ‚Äî Controlled or uncontrolled front panel; fires on every settled detent.
- `itemWidth / itemHeight / perspective`: number (default 160 / 200 / 1000) ‚Äî Panel geometry; the ring radius derives from width and count.
- `sensitivity`: number (default 0.35) ‚Äî Drag ratio in degrees per pixel.
- `getLabel`: (index) => string ‚Äî Names panels for the live announcer.

Notes:
- Only the fronted panel is interactive; the rest are inert and aria-hidden.
- Under reduced motion the drum becomes a flat scroll-snap row with identical semantics.

### Wavefield (KQ-032) ‚Äî `wavefield`
Ambient physics, drawn on canvas.

Ambient physics, drawn on canvas ‚Äî interference lattices, drifting contours, breathing grids, and particle fields that pause when unseen.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/wavefield`

Props:
- `variant`: "interference" | "contour" | "lattice" | "drift" (default "interference") ‚Äî Which field is drawn.
- `speed / density / opacity`: number (default 0.5 / 0.5 / 1) ‚Äî Clock rate, geometry density (capped), and canvas-layer opacity.
- `children`: ReactNode ‚Äî Overlay slot above the canvas ‚Äî it carries all the semantics.

Notes:
- Colors resolve from your theme tokens and re-resolve on theme flips; the loop pauses when hidden or offscreen.
- Under reduced motion exactly one designed frame is drawn ‚Äî the loop never starts.

### Phase Switch (KQ-033) ‚Äî `phase-switch`
Day to night in one clean sweep.

Day to night in one clean sweep ‚Äî a framework-agnostic theme toggle that reveals the new theme as a circle growing from the button, via the View Transitions API.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/phase-switch`

Props:
- `checked / onCheckedChange`: boolean / (next) => void ‚Äî Controlled only ‚Äî flip your own theme class inside the callback; the component owns zero theme logic.
- `size / label`: "sm" | "md" | "lg" / string (default "md" / "Toggle theme") ‚Äî Control size and accessible name.

Notes:
- Add `::view-transition-old(root), ::view-transition-new(root) { animation: none }` to your CSS so the clip reveal isn't fought by the default crossfade.
- Unsupported browsers and reduced motion get an instant flip with an icon crossfade.

### Kinetic Gallery (KQ-035) ‚Äî `kinetic-gallery`
Throw the strip; the nearest frame finds the rail.

Grab the strip and throw it ‚Äî release carries momentum that decays and snaps the nearest frame to the rail, with prev/next and dot controls.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/kinetic-gallery`

Props:
- `children`: ReactNode ‚Äî Each direct child is a slide; widths are measured for snapping.
- `gap`: number (default 16) ‚Äî Pixel gap between slides.
- `align`: "start" | "center" (default "start") ‚Äî Where the active slide settles against the rail.
- `aria-label`: string ‚Äî Names the gallery region for assistive tech.

Notes:
- Arrow keys step slides, Home and End jump to the ends; a live region announces the current frame.
- Under reduced motion it becomes a native scroll-snap strip ‚Äî direct swipe, no thrown momentum.

### Tile Grid (KQ-036) ‚Äî `tile-grid`
Drag a tile; the grid reflows around it.

Drag a tile and the grid reflows around it ‚Äî the others FLIP-glide into their new slots, with a full keyboard lift-move-drop for reordering without a mouse.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/tile-grid`

Props:
- `tiles`: { id: string; content: ReactNode }[] ‚Äî The tiles in their initial order; identity drives the FLIP.
- `columns`: number (default 3) ‚Äî Grid column count.
- `onOrderChange`: (ids: string[]) => void ‚Äî Fires with the new id order after a drop or keyboard move.
- `aria-label`: string ‚Äî Names the reorderable list.

Notes:
- Keyboard: Space lifts a tile, arrows move it (left/right by one, up/down by a row), Space drops, Escape cancels.
- Under reduced motion tiles snap to their new positions ‚Äî dragging still tracks 1:1, without the glide.

### Segmented Control (KQ-037) ‚Äî `segmented-control`
A thumb that glides to the chosen segment.

A compact value picker whose raised thumb glides between segments on snap ‚Äî radio-group semantics, so it selects a value rather than switching panels.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/segmented-control`

Props:
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange`: string / string / (value) => void ‚Äî Controlled or uncontrolled selection.
- `name / label`: string / ReactNode ‚Äî Hidden native radios for form posts; a visible group label (or pass aria-label).
- `size`: "sm" | "md" (default "md") ‚Äî Control height and type scale.
- `Item.value / Item.disabled`: string / boolean ‚Äî Segment identity and per-segment disabling.

Notes:
- Full APG radio keyboard: arrows move and select (wrapping), Home and End jump, Space selects.
- Under reduced motion the thumb appears under the active segment instantly ‚Äî no travel, color only.

### Triage Deck (KQ-038) ‚Äî `triage-deck`
Flick to accept, flick to reject.

A decision stack you flick through ‚Äî throw a card right to accept or left to reject, a velocity-projected release commits, and the next card rises from behind.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/triage-deck`

Props:
- `cards`: { id: string; content: ReactNode }[] ‚Äî The queue; the top card is interactive, the rest peek behind.
- `onDecide`: (id, decision: "accept" | "reject") => void ‚Äî Fires when a card commits in either direction.
- `onEmpty`: () => void ‚Äî Fires when the last card is decided.
- `acceptLabel / rejectLabel`: string / string ‚Äî Button and affordance labels.

Notes:
- Arrow keys decide (left rejects, right accepts) and buttons drive it pointer-free; a live region announces each decision.
- Under reduced motion cards cross-fade instead of flying off ‚Äî dragging still tracks, without the throw.

### Spark Chart (KQ-039) ‚Äî `spark-chart`
A trace that draws itself, then reads out.

A compact line or area chart that draws itself on arrival, then lets you scrub a crosshair that snaps to the nearest point and reads its value.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/spark-chart`

Props:
- `data`: number[] | { x: number | string; y: number }[] ‚Äî Bare numbers get index labels; objects carry their own x.
- `variant`: "line" | "area" (default "line") ‚Äî Stroke only, or a filled area under the curve.
- `height / format`: number / (y) => string ‚Äî Chart height in px and value formatting for the readout.
- `label`: string ‚Äî Accessible name and summary for the trace.

Notes:
- Focus and arrow-key to move the crosshair; Home and End jump; the active point is announced.
- Under reduced motion the trace renders fully drawn ‚Äî scrubbing still works, without the draw-on.

### Radial Bars (KQ-040) ‚Äî `radial-bars`
Bars that radiate from a hub.

A polar bar chart ‚Äî wedges radiate from a hub, each growing to its value on the glide spring; hover or focus a wedge to brighten it and read its value in the hub.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/radial-bars`

Props:
- `data`: { label: string; value: number; color?: string }[] ‚Äî One wedge per entry; value drives its radial length.
- `max`: number ‚Äî Value that reaches the outer radius; defaults to the data max.
- `size / format`: number / (value) => string ‚Äî Diameter in px and value formatting for the readout.
- `aria-label`: string ‚Äî Names the chart region.

Notes:
- Every wedge is a focusable data point announcing its label and value, so the chart is operable by keyboard.
- Under reduced motion the wedges render at full length instantly ‚Äî the hover/focus highlight is color only.

### Coverflow (KQ-041) ‚Äî `coverflow`
Covers that bank away in perspective.

A cover gallery in perspective ‚Äî the active card faces you while its neighbors bank away and recede, and any card you steer to glides to the front.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/coverflow`

Props:
- `children`: ReactNode ‚Äî Each direct child is a cover.
- `index / defaultIndex / onIndexChange`: number / number / (index) => void ‚Äî Controlled or uncontrolled active cover.
- `aria-label`: string ‚Äî Names the gallery region.

Notes:
- Drag, wheel, click a side cover, or arrow-key to steer; a live region announces the active item.
- Under reduced motion it flattens to a native scroll-snap row ‚Äî no perspective, same controls.

### Parallax Scene (KQ-042) ‚Äî `parallax-scene`
Layers that part around the pointer.

A depth-layered diorama that parades its layers past the pointer ‚Äî each sits at its own depth, the whole scene tilts toward your cursor and drifts back on leave.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/parallax-scene`

Props:
- `children`: ReactNode ‚Äî ParallaxLayer elements, back to front (max ~4).
- `maxTilt`: number (default 8) ‚Äî Degrees the scene rotates toward the pointer.
- `Layer.depth`: number ‚Äî 0 is far and moves least; 1 is near and moves most.

Notes:
- The parallax is decorative ‚Äî layer content stays in normal flow and fully focusable.
- Under reduced motion the layers render flat and stationary at their neutral position.

### Tether Rope (KQ-043) ‚Äî `tether-rope`
Grab the cord; Verlet does the rest.

A hanging rope you grab and swing ‚Äî a Verlet chain with gravity and length constraints, so it whips, goes taut, and settles like real cord.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/tether-rope`

Props:
- `nodes`: number (default 12) ‚Äî Segment nodes, clamped to 4‚Äì16.
- `anchor`: "top" | "ends" (default "top") ‚Äî Pin the first node, or both ends like a slung cable.
- `height`: number (default 280) ‚Äî Stage height in px.

Notes:
- Drag any point to swing the rope; a Reset button re-drops it. The loop pauses offscreen and when the tab is hidden.
- Under reduced motion it renders a static hanging catenary ‚Äî no simulation.

### Pendulum Wave (KQ-044) ‚Äî `pendulum-wave`
Periods that part, then realign.

A rank of pendulums whose periods step apart ‚Äî released together they drift through traveling waves and snap back into alignment on a fixed cycle.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/pendulum-wave`

Props:
- `count`: number (default 12) ‚Äî Pendulums, clamped to 4‚Äì20.
- `amplitude / cycleSeconds / baseOscillations`: number ‚Äî Swing angle, realign-cycle length, and the longest pendulum's swing count.
- `height`: number (default 260) ‚Äî Stage height in px.

Notes:
- Motion is analytic ‚Äî each bob follows a closed-form cosine, so the realign cycle is exact. Restart re-aligns them.
- Under reduced motion it renders one static fanned frame ‚Äî no loop.

### Rubber Sheet (KQ-045) ‚Äî `rubber-sheet`
Pull the membrane; it rings back.

A taut mesh you pull toward the pointer with a smooth falloff; release and the vertices fire home, ringing through equilibrium twice before they settle.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/rubber-sheet`

Props:
- `columns / rows`: number ‚Äî Mesh density; columns default 9, rows default 7.
- `height`: number (default 260) ‚Äî Stage height in px.
- `aria-label`: string ‚Äî Names the surface.

Notes:
- The release rides the recoil spring, so the sheet overshoots and settles in two visible bounces.
- Under reduced motion the mesh deforms 1:1 while dragging but snaps flat on release ‚Äî no ripple.

### Iron Filings (KQ-046) ‚Äî `iron-filings`
A field that aligns to your cursor.

A canvas lattice of dash filings that align to the cursor like iron around a magnetic pole ‚Äî near the pointer they snap radial and brighten, far off they relax and dim.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/iron-filings`

Props:
- `spacing`: number (default 26) ‚Äî Pixels between filings in the grid (clamped 16‚Äì48).
- `height`: number (default 320) ‚Äî Stage height in px when used standalone.
- `children`: ReactNode ‚Äî Overlay content rendered above the field.

Notes:
- The canvas loop pauses offscreen and when the tab is hidden; colors re-resolve on a theme flip.
- Under reduced motion it renders one static lattice frame ‚Äî no loop, no cursor response.

### Swarm Field (KQ-047) ‚Äî `swarm-field`
A flock that scatters from your cursor.

A canvas flock of boids that murmur with emergent flocking and scatter from the cursor, then regroup ‚Äî separation, alignment, cohesion, and flee, on a spatial hash.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/swarm-field`

Props:
- `count`: number (default 80) ‚Äî Boid count, clamped to 10‚Äì100.
- `height`: number (default 320) ‚Äî Stage height in px when used standalone.
- `children`: ReactNode ‚Äî Overlay content rendered above the field.

Notes:
- Neighbor queries run on a spatial hash, so the flock stays near O(n); the loop pauses offscreen and when hidden.
- Under reduced motion it renders one settled frame ‚Äî no loop, no scatter.

### Magnetic Cursor (KQ-048) ‚Äî `magnetic-cursor`
Targets that reach for the cursor.

A pointer layer for fine cursors ‚Äî a drift-lagged spotlight trails the cursor and any MagneticTarget leans toward it, then springs back; inert on touch and reduced motion.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/magnetic-cursor`

Props:
- `MagneticCursor.size`: number (default 28) ‚Äî Spotlight follower diameter in px.
- `MagneticTarget.strength`: number (default 0.35) ‚Äî Fraction of the pointer offset a target follows.
- `MagneticTarget.radius`: number (default 90) ‚Äî Activation radius in px around the target.

Notes:
- Wrap real controls in MagneticTarget ‚Äî the transform layer never touches focus or clicks.
- Active only on (pointer:fine) with motion allowed; otherwise children render untouched.

### Aurora Ribbon (KQ-049) ‚Äî `aurora-ribbon`
Light ribbons that lean toward the cursor.

Slow sinusoidal ribbons of light that undulate across the field like an aurora and bend toward the pointer ‚Äî a calm, luminous canvas backdrop.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/aurora-ribbon`

Props:
- `bands`: number (default 3) ‚Äî Number of ribbons, clamped to 1‚Äì6.
- `height`: number (default 320) ‚Äî Stage height in px when used standalone.
- `children`: ReactNode ‚Äî Overlay content rendered above the field.

Notes:
- Ribbons bloom where they overlap (additive compositing); the loop pauses offscreen and when the tab is hidden.
- Under reduced motion it renders one static frame ‚Äî no undulation, no pointer response.

### Point Globe (KQ-050) ‚Äî `point-globe`
A dotted planet you spin.

A globe of points on a Fibonacci sphere that drifts on its own and spins under your drag, carrying angular momentum ‚Äî near points brighten, the far side dims.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/point-globe`

Props:
- `points`: number (default 520) ‚Äî Dots on the sphere, clamped to 120‚Äì700.
- `autoRotate`: boolean (default true) ‚Äî Idle drift when not being dragged.
- `height`: number (default 340) ‚Äî Stage height in px.

Notes:
- Drag to spin; release carries momentum that decays back to the idle drift. The loop pauses offscreen and when hidden.
- Under reduced motion it renders a static globe ‚Äî no drift, no momentum.

### Voronoi Shatter (KQ-051) ‚Äî `voronoi-shatter`
Tap and the tessellation shatters, then reseals.

A Voronoi tessellation that fractures outward from your tap ‚Äî shards fly, rotate, and fade, then ring back into place and reseal on the recoil spring.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/voronoi-shatter`

Props:
- `cells`: number (default 28) ‚Äî Voronoi seed sites, clamped to 8‚Äì60.
- `height`: number (default 280) ‚Äî Stage height in px.
- `aria-label`: string ‚Äî Names the surface.

Notes:
- Cells are a real Voronoi built from half-plane clipping, computed once; the shard transforms repaint imperatively from one progress spring.
- Under reduced motion a tap leaves the tessellation whole ‚Äî no shatter.

### Comet Cursor (KQ-052) ‚Äî `comet-cursor`
A comet that chases the pointer.

A contained field where the pointer drags a luminous comet ‚Äî a bright head with a tapering tail that thickens with speed and collapses when you rest.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/comet-cursor`

Props:
- `trail`: number (default 28) ‚Äî Trail sample count, clamped to 8‚Äì64.
- `height`: number (default 320) ‚Äî Stage height in px.
- `children`: ReactNode ‚Äî Overlay content rendered above the field.

Notes:
- The tail widens with pointer speed and collapses into the head at rest; the loop pauses offscreen and when hidden.
- Under reduced motion it renders one static frame ‚Äî no trail, no pointer response.

### Ripple Surface (KQ-053) ‚Äî `ripple-surface`
Tap and wavefronts interfere.

Tap the surface and a wavefront propagates out; tap again and the rings overlap into visible interference ‚Äî a calm canvas pond that answers every touch.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/ripple-surface`

Props:
- `height`: number (default 320) ‚Äî Stage height in px.
- `children`: ReactNode ‚Äî Overlay content rendered above the field.

Notes:
- Concurrent ripples are pooled and capped; crests reinforce where wavefronts cross. The loop pauses offscreen and when hidden.
- Under reduced motion it renders one calm static frame ‚Äî a tap does not propagate.

### Flow Diagram (KQ-054) ‚Äî `flow-diagram`
Quantity, flowing between nodes.

A Sankey flow ‚Äî nodes in columns, links sized to their value with a marching-dash current, and a hover that traces a node's whole upstream and downstream path.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/flow-diagram`

Props:
- `nodes`: { id: string; label: string; column: number }[] ‚Äî Graph nodes with their column placement.
- `links`: { source: string; target: string; value: number }[] ‚Äî Flows; value drives link thickness.
- `height`: number (default 300) ‚Äî Stage height in px.

Notes:
- Hover or focus a node to trace its connected path; an sr-only summary lists every flow.
- Under reduced motion the dashes stop marching ‚Äî the highlight is color only.

### Timeline Spine (KQ-055) ‚Äî `timeline-spine`
A playhead that rides your scroll.

A scroll-linked timeline ‚Äî a playhead rides the spine as you scroll, the spine fills behind it, and each event pops in as the head reaches it.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/timeline-spine`

Props:
- `events`: { title: string; detail?: ReactNode; marker?: ReactNode }[] ‚Äî Ordered events rendered along the spine.
- `aria-label`: string ‚Äî Names the timeline region.

Notes:
- Events are a semantic ordered list, readable regardless of motion; the spine and playhead are decorative.
- Under reduced motion everything renders in its final state ‚Äî no scroll-driven animation.

### Pull to Refresh (KQ-056) ‚Äî `pull-to-refresh`
Pull past the detent; rows cascade in.

Over-pull the list past a calibrated detent and release ‚Äî it holds to refresh, then settles back as the fresh rows cascade in.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/pull-to-refresh`

Props:
- `onRefresh`: () => Promise<void> | void ‚Äî Called when a past-detent pull is released.
- `children`: ReactNode ‚Äî The scrollable content.
- `threshold`: number (default 72) ‚Äî Pixels past which release triggers a refresh.

Notes:
- Only engages when the list is scrolled to the top; a focusable Refresh button keeps it operable without a pointer.
- Under reduced motion the pull eases home without overshoot and the new rows fade in once ‚Äî no cascade.

### Orbit Menu (KQ-057) ‚Äî `orbit-menu`
A dial of options that snaps to a detent.

Items ring a hub and rotate on a weighted dial ‚Äî flick the ring and it flings, then snaps the nearest item into the active detent at the top.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/orbit-menu`

Props:
- `items`: { id: string; label: string; icon?: ReactNode }[] ‚Äî Options placed evenly around the ring.
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange`: string / string / (id) => void ‚Äî Controlled or uncontrolled active item.
- `size`: number (default 260) ‚Äî Ring diameter in px.

Notes:
- Drag to spin, click an item, or arrow-key to rotate; a live region announces the active item.
- Under reduced motion selection snaps to the detent instantly ‚Äî no fling momentum.

### Spark Burst (KQ-058) ‚Äî `spark-burst`
Restraint as celebration.

A calibrated celebration, not confetti ‚Äî call fire() and a tight radial burst of hairline rays and a fast ring shoots out and fades, monochrome with a signal glint.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/spark-burst`

Props:
- `ref.fire(opts?)`: (opts?: { rays?: number }) => void ‚Äî Imperative handle ‚Äî call it to emit one burst.
- `rays / spread`: number / number ‚Äî Ray count (default 12) and travel distance in px (default 40).
- `color`: string (default "var(--signal)") ‚Äî Ray color.

Notes:
- Get a ref of type SparkBurstHandle and call fire(); rapid fires stack and clean themselves up.
- Under reduced motion fire() gives a single centered pulse ‚Äî no radiating rays.

### Heart Tap (KQ-059) ‚Äî `heart-tap`
Pop, sparks, and a rolling count.

A like that celebrates ‚Äî the heart squashes then pops, a tight ring of sparks bursts once, and the count rolls up; un-liking deflates and rolls back down.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/heart-tap`

Props:
- `liked / defaultLiked / onChange`: boolean / boolean / (state) => void ‚Äî Controlled or uncontrolled liked state; onChange gets { liked, count }.
- `count / defaultCount`: number / number ‚Äî Controlled or uncontrolled count, clamped at zero.
- `size`: number (default 28) ‚Äî Heart glyph size in px.

Notes:
- A real toggle button ‚Äî Space/Enter like, aria-pressed and the count are announced.
- Under reduced motion the fill switches and the count swaps instantly ‚Äî no pop, sparks, or roll.

### Reaction Fly (KQ-060) ‚Äî `reaction-fly`
Reactions that bloom, then fly.

Press to bloom a reaction picker; choosing one sends a copy flying up ‚Äî rising, drifting, and fading on its own jittered path ‚Äî while its tally ticks up.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/reaction-fly`

Props:
- `reactions`: { id: string; label: string; node?: ReactNode }[] ‚Äî The pickable reactions; defaults to a curated set.
- `onReact`: (id: string) => void ‚Äî Fires with the reaction id on each selection.
- `aria-label`: string ‚Äî Names the trigger.

Notes:
- Full menu semantics ‚Äî the trigger has aria-haspopup, items are menuitems, Escape closes, and each reaction is announced.
- Under reduced motion the picker opens without stagger and selecting bumps the tally with no fly-up.

### Facet Cube (KQ-071) ‚Äî `facet-cube`
Six faces, one cube, snap detents.

A six-face content cube with real CSS geometry ‚Äî drag to rotate with per-pixel resistance, release and it snaps both axes to the nearest 90¬∞ detent, or walk faces with the arrow keys; the facing face is announced on settle.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/facet-cube`

Props:
- `faces`: ReactNode[] ‚Äî Up to six face plates; missing faces render empty.
- `labels`: string[] ‚Äî Per-face names for the screen-reader announcement.
- `size`: number (default 220) ‚Äî Cube edge in px; the wrapper pads for mid-turn corners.
- `defaultFace / onFaceChange`: number / (index) => void ‚Äî Starting face and the settle callback.

Notes:
- A focusable group with aria-roledescription ‚Äî arrows rotate one detent, Home returns to face one, and the facing face is announced politely.
- Under reduced motion the cube renders flat: one face at a time, instant swaps, same keys and announcements.

### Prism Flip (KQ-072) ‚Äî `prism-flip`
Three panels on one rolling prism.

A triangular prism that rolls toward the viewer between three panels ‚Äî tap to advance, drag vertically against the detents, or step with the arrow keys; true equilateral geometry with edge shading.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/prism-flip`

Props:
- `panels`: ReactNode[] ‚Äî The three faces; extra entries are ignored.
- `index / defaultIndex / onIndexChange`: number / number / (index) => void ‚Äî Controlled or uncontrolled facing panel.
- `labels`: string[] ‚Äî Per-panel names for the announcement.
- `height`: number (default 128) ‚Äî Panel height in px; clearance is derived from it.

Notes:
- A focusable rotator ‚Äî arrows step panels, Home returns to the first, and the facing panel is announced politely.
- Under reduced motion the prism renders one flat panel with instant crossfade swaps.

### Coin Toggle (KQ-073) ‚Äî `coin-toggle`
Heads on, tails off.

A switch struck as a coin ‚Äî it flips 180¬∞ in true perspective on every toggle, always forward, and lands with an edge-wobble on the recoil spring; faces are yours to mint.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/coin-toggle`

Props:
- `checked / defaultChecked / onCheckedChange`: boolean / boolean / (checked) => void ‚Äî Controlled or uncontrolled switch state.
- `size`: number (default 56) ‚Äî Coin diameter in px.
- `faces`: { on: ReactNode; off: ReactNode } ‚Äî Custom face artwork; defaults to minted glyphs.
- `aria-label`: string ‚Äî Required accessible name for the bare switch.

Notes:
- A real role=switch button ‚Äî Space and Enter flip it, aria-checked tracks state, and focus-visible rings are never removed.
- Under reduced motion the faces crossfade with no flip or wobble ‚Äî the accent change carries the state.

### Dice Roll (KQ-074) ‚Äî `dice-roll`
A die that always answers.

Hold to charge ‚Äî the die swells and shivers while an arc fills ‚Äî then release to tumble two full turns and land on a face you chose: outcomes come from a prop or a fixed sequence, never chance.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/dice-roll`

Props:
- `value / onRoll`: number / (value) => void ‚Äî Controlled outcome for the next roll, and the landing callback.
- `sequence`: number[] ‚Äî Uncontrolled outcomes, cycled in order ‚Äî deterministic by design.
- `size`: number (default 88) ‚Äî Die edge in px.

Notes:
- A real button ‚Äî hold Space or Enter to charge, release to roll; Escape aborts a charge; the result is announced politely.
- Under reduced motion there is no shiver or tumble ‚Äî release swaps straight to the outcome face and announces it.

### Gimbal Dial (KQ-075) ‚Äî `gimbal-dial`
Two rings, two channels, one hub.

A two-axis picker built as nested rings in perspective ‚Äî spin the outer band for one value and the inner for the other; flicks carry momentum, releases settle on detents, and a hub reads both channels live.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/gimbal-dial`

Props:
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange`: { yaw, pitch } / same / (value) => void ‚Äî Controlled or uncontrolled 0‚Äì100 per axis; fires once per settled detent.
- `step`: number (default 5) ‚Äî Detent size in value units.
- `size`: number (default 200) ‚Äî Outer ring diameter in px.
- `axisLabels`: { yaw: string; pitch: string } ‚Äî Accessible names for the two slider bands.

Notes:
- Each ring is an independent role=slider ‚Äî arrows step it, Home and End jump, and aria-valuenow tracks the detents.
- Under reduced motion the rings render flat and drags update values directly with no momentum.

### Rolodex List (KQ-076) ‚Äî `rolodex-list`
A wheel of records with a crest.

Records on a vertical wheel ‚Äî drag, scroll a notch at a time, or arrow through; cards flip over the crest, the front record carries the accent, and Enter pulls it. A selection control, not a display drum.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/rolodex-list`

Props:
- `items`: { id, content, label? }[] ‚Äî The records on the wheel.
- `index / defaultIndex / onIndexChange`: number / number / (index) => void ‚Äî Controlled or uncontrolled front record; change fires on settle.
- `onSelect`: (id, index) => void ‚Äî Explicit activation ‚Äî Enter, Space, or a tap on the front card.
- `height / loop`: number / boolean (default 260 / true) ‚Äî Viewport height in px, and whether the wheel wraps.

Notes:
- A real listbox ‚Äî aria-activedescendant tracks the front record, arrows rotate one step, and the wheel only captures scroll while the pointer is over it.
- Under reduced motion it renders a flat three-row list with instant swaps and the same semantics.

### Wheel Picker (KQ-077) ‚Äî `wheel-picker`
Options on a drum, settled by detent.

A value picker built as a recessed drum ‚Äî options curve beneath a fixed selection window, drags carry momentum into detents, every landing ticks the window caps, and letter keys typeahead straight to an option.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/wheel-picker`

Props:
- `options`: { value: string; label: string }[] ‚Äî The drum's rows.
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange`: string / string / (value) => void ‚Äî Controlled or uncontrolled selection; fires once per settle.
- `visibleRows / height`: 3 | 5 | 7 / number (default 5 / 200) ‚Äî Window size and control height.
- `aria-label / aria-labelledby`: string ‚Äî One is required ‚Äî it is a bare form control.

Notes:
- A real listbox ‚Äî arrows and Page keys step rows, Home and End jump, letters typeahead, and the wheel only captures scroll over the control.
- Under reduced motion the drum renders as a flat column with instant swaps and the same window styling.

### Flip Mosaic (KQ-078) ‚Äî `flip-mosaic`
One grid, two boards, a traveling flip.

Two boards on one grid ‚Äî every tile carries an A face and a B face, and toggling sweeps a cascade of Y-flips across the mosaic from your chosen origin, always inside the choreography budget.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/flip-mosaic`

Props:
- `tiles`: { a: ReactNode; b: ReactNode }[] ‚Äî Each tile's two faces.
- `side / defaultSide / onSideChange`: "a" | "b" / same / (side) => void ‚Äî Controlled or uncontrolled facing board.
- `origin`: "start" | "end" | "center" (default "start") ‚Äî Where the cascade wave begins.
- `columns / tileAspect / gap`: number / number / number (default 4 / 1 / 8) ‚Äî Grid shape.

Notes:
- Interactive mode is a single role=switch button ‚Äî Space and Enter flip the board and the settled side is announced.
- Under reduced motion there is no cascade or 3D ‚Äî the whole board crossfades at once.

### Orrery (KQ-079) ‚Äî `orrery`
An orbital system with a focus slot.

Bodies orbit a hub on inclined elliptical paths ‚Äî inner rings run faster, the whole system pauses when unwatched, and clicking a body flies it forward into a focus card until Escape sends it home.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/orrery`

Props:
- `items`: { id, label, node? }[] ‚Äî Up to eight orbiting bodies; default faces are minted discs.
- `hub / detail`: ReactNode / (item) => ReactNode ‚Äî The center piece and the focus-card renderer.
- `radius / inclineDeg / period`: number / number / number (default 120 / 24 / 36) ‚Äî Orbit geometry and the outer revolution time in seconds.
- `onFocusChange`: (id | null) => void ‚Äî Fires when a body is captured or released.

Notes:
- Every body is a real button ‚Äî Enter captures it, Escape releases, and captures are announced politely; the orbit pauses under keyboard focus.
- The loop pauses offscreen and when the tab hides, with the clock rebased on resume; under reduced motion the system rests at its phase positions and captures swap instantly.

### Command Deck (KB-101) ‚Äî `command-deck`
A palette that arms before it fires.

A command palette that arms before it fires ‚Äî a deliberate fill sweeps the selected row before execution, so destructive commands never feel accidental.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/command-deck`

Props:
- `commands`: DeckCommand[] ‚Äî { id, label, hint?, icon?, section?, keywords?, destructive?, run } per command.
- `open / defaultOpen / onOpenChange`: boolean / boolean / (open) => void ‚Äî Controlled or uncontrolled visibility.
- `hotkey`: boolean (default true) ‚Äî Global ‚åòK / Ctrl+K toggle listener.
- `inline / portal`: boolean (default false / true) ‚Äî Inline renders just the panel (docs/demos); portal overlays into body with focus trap and scroll lock.
- `recentKey`: string (default "kinetiq-deck-recents") ‚Äî localStorage key for the five most recent command ids.

Notes:
- Full combobox semantics with focus trap and restore; Escape mid-arm cancels the sweep.
- Under reduced motion the arming sweep becomes an instant fill with a deliberate 120ms pause.

### Access Panel (KB-102) ‚Äî `access-panel`
Sign-in that unlocks like a vault.

Sign-in that unlocks like a vault ‚Äî steps ride a gantry rail, OTP digits drop into their cells, and the right code throws a breaker bolt open.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/access-panel`

Props:
- `expectedCode`: string (default "246810") ‚Äî The six-digit code that throws the bolt.
- `onVerify`: (code: string) => void ‚Äî Fires with the entered code on every attempt.
- `onComplete`: (email: string) => void ‚Äî Fires once when the correct code lands.
- `email`: string ‚Äî Prefills the email field.

Notes:
- One hidden input with autocomplete="one-time-code" drives the six visual cells ‚Äî the accessibility-correct OTP pattern.
- Under reduced motion the rail slides become fades and the rejection nudge is skipped; the alert still announces.

### Signal Center (KB-103) ‚Äî `signal-center`
An inbox that files itself.

An inbox that files itself ‚Äî signals arrive on the conveyor, swipe away with real velocity, and clear-all sweeps the floor in a cascade.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/signal-center`

Props:
- `signals / defaultSignals / onSignalsChange`: Signal[] / Signal[] / (signals) => void ‚Äî Controlled or uncontrolled feed; Signal = { id, source, title, detail?, time, read? }.
- `onArchive`: (signal: Signal) => void ‚Äî Fires per archived signal, including clear-all.
- `title`: string (default "Signals") ‚Äî Header title next to the live unread readout.

Notes:
- Archiving is keyboard-reachable via a per-row button; swiping is the fast path, not the only path.
- Under reduced motion arrivals and exits become fades and clear-all loses its stagger.

### Media Console (KB-104) ‚Äî `media-console`
A media island that unfolds into a console.

A media island that unfolds into a console ‚Äî the pill morphs open into transport, a caliper timeline, and a scope-scrubbed volume.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/media-console`

Props:
- `tracks`: Track[] ‚Äî { id, title, artist, duration } ‚Äî playback is simulated; the JSDoc shows wiring a real audio element.
- `defaultExpanded`: boolean (default false) ‚Äî Start as the pill or the full console.

Notes:
- Complete keyboard transport; Escape collapses the console back to the pill.
- Under reduced motion the morph becomes a crossfade and the waveform holds still.

### Checkout Receipt (KB-105) ‚Äî `checkout-receipt`
Payment that prints its proof.

Payment that prints its proof ‚Äî hold to pay, then a receipt feeds out of the slot line by line, tears off, and takes a PAID stamp.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/checkout-receipt`

Props:
- `items`: ReceiptItem[] ‚Äî Line items ({ name, price }) summed into the total.
- `currency`: string (default "$") ‚Äî Price prefix.
- `onPay`: () => void ‚Äî Fires when the hold completes.

Notes:
- The receipt is real text in a definition list; only the barcode is decorative.
- Under reduced motion the receipt appears whole and the stamp fades in.

### Intake Tray (KB-106) ‚Äî `intake-tray`
Files land, physically.

Files land, physically ‚Äî dropped files fall into the tray with a squash, shingle into a stack, fill their gauge rings, and slide to the processed rail.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/intake-tray`

Props:
- `onFiles`: (files: File[]) => void ‚Äî Receives each accepted batch.
- `accept / maxFiles`: string / number (default ‚Äî / 6) ‚Äî Accepted types hint and tray capacity.
- `simulate`: boolean (default true) ‚Äî Hash-driven demo progress; the JSDoc documents wiring real uploads.
- `ref`: Ref<IntakeTrayHandle> ‚Äî Imperative addFiles() for programmatic intake.

Notes:
- The drop zone is a real button with a labeled native file input ‚Äî fully keyboard operable.
- Under reduced motion chips stack neatly with fades; the gauge rings still fill.

### Readout Grid (KB-107) ‚Äî `readout-grid`
Dashboard stats as instrument cards.

Dashboard stats as instrument cards ‚Äî a carry-rolling counter, a self-drawing sparkline, a split-flap delta, and a needle gauge, mounted with one cascade.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/readout-grid`

Props:
- `metrics`: ReadoutGridMetrics ‚Äî Optional full override of the four cards: counter, sparkline, flap, and gauge shapes.

Notes:
- Each card is an article whose label reads naturally to screen readers; chart visuals carry sr-only values.
- Under reduced motion cards render in place, the sparkline arrives fully drawn, and the needle jumps.

### Launch Checklist (KB-108) ‚Äî `launch-checklist`
Setup steps that stamp themselves done.

Onboarding checklist where ticks draw themselves, finished steps strike through and settle to the bottom, and completing the set lands a CALIBRATED stamp.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/launch-checklist`

Props:
- `steps`: ChecklistStep[] ‚Äî Step definitions: id, title, optional description.
- `completed / defaultCompleted`: string[] ‚Äî Controlled or uncontrolled set of completed step ids.
- `onCompletedChange`: (completed: string[]) => void ‚Äî Fires on every toggle with the new completed set.
- `onComplete`: () => void ‚Äî Fires once when the final step completes.

Notes:
- Rows are native checkboxes under the hood ‚Äî the whole block is keyboard and screen-reader operable.
- Under reduced motion rows reorder instantly and the stamp fades in; the progress track still reports value via ARIA.

### Iris Menu (KB-109) ‚Äî `iris-menu`
Actions bloom from where you pressed.

Actions bloom from where you pressed ‚Äî items launch from the trigger's center to seats along the freest quadrant's arc, and fold back in on close.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/iris-menu`

Props:
- `items`: IrisMenuItem[] ‚Äî Up to six: { id, icon, label, onSelect, disabled?, destructive? }.
- `placement`: "auto" | quadrant (default "auto") ‚Äî Auto measures the viewport and blooms into the roomiest quadrant.
- `radius`: number (default 84) ‚Äî Distance from trigger center to each seat.

Notes:
- Arrow keys rotate focus around the ring; Escape closes and restores the trigger.
- Under reduced motion items fade in already seated ‚Äî no travel.

### Overflow Rail (KB-110) ‚Äî `overflow-rail`
More actions, sprung from the dots.

More actions, sprung from the dots ‚Äî the pill rail morphs open inline while primaries yield with a recoil part and secondaries cascade in.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/overflow-rail`

Props:
- `primary / secondary`: RailAction[] ‚Äî Always-visible actions and the set revealed by the ‚ãØ trigger.
- `open / defaultOpen / onOpenChange`: boolean / boolean / (open) => void ‚Äî Controlled or uncontrolled expansion.
- `label`: string (default "Actions") ‚Äî Accessible toolbar name.

Notes:
- One toolbar: arrows rove across every visible action; Escape collapses and refocuses the trigger.
- Under reduced motion the width jumps and secondaries fade in place.

### Beacon (KB-111) ‚Äî `beacon`
One capsule, every live activity.

One capsule, every live activity ‚Äî the shell morphs between timer, upload, call, and now-playing views with blur crossfades, corners pinned.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/beacon`

Props:
- `activity`: BeaconActivity | null ‚Äî Discriminated union: timer (endsAt), upload (progress), call (accept/decline), playing (title/artist); null collapses to a standby pill.
- `expanded / defaultExpanded / onExpandedChange`: boolean / boolean / (expanded) => void ‚Äî Controlled or uncontrolled detail row.
- `onDismiss`: () => void ‚Äî Renders a dismiss action in the detail row.

Notes:
- Call buttons are reachable while collapsed; activity changes announce once, debounced.
- Under reduced motion the shell resizes instantly and the loops hold still.

### Field Report (KB-112) ‚Äî `field-report`
Feedback that files itself.

Feedback that files itself ‚Äî a notch rating with a gliding indicator, a growing note field, and a submit that folds the form into a slot under a LOGGED stamp.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/field-report`

Props:
- `onSubmit`: ({ rating, note }) => void ‚Äî Fires on valid submit; rating is 1‚Äì5.
- `prompt / endLabels`: string / [string, string] (default "How did it feel?" / ["Rough","Dialed"]) ‚Äî The question and the rail's end labels.
- `resetAfterMs`: number ‚Äî Fades a fresh form back in after filing.

Notes:
- The rating is a real radiogroup ‚Äî arrows move the indicator, and empty submits nudge with a role=alert line.
- Under reduced motion the filing collapse becomes a fade and the stamp lands statically.

### Not Found (KB-113) ‚Äî `not-found`
Sweep complete. Sector empty.

Sweep complete, sector empty ‚Äî a radar arc scans a quiet grid while the 404 numeral deciphers itself, with home and command-deck exits.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/not-found`

Props:
- `homeHref`: string (default "/") ‚Äî Return-to-base link target.
- `onCommandDeck`: () => void ‚Äî Renders the ghost command-deck action when provided.
- `code / message`: string (default "404" / sector message) ‚Äî The cipher numeral and the rising subline.

Notes:
- The heading carries real text for screen readers; the cipher layer and radar are decorative.
- Under reduced motion the sweep freezes mid-arc and the numeral renders plainly.

### Forecast Card (KB-114) ‚Äî `forecast-card`
Every vote moves every bar.

Every vote moves every bar ‚Äî one commit renormalizes the whole field while percentages carry-roll and the leader tick migrates to the new front-runner.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/forecast-card`

Props:
- `question / options`: string / ForecastOption[] ‚Äî 2‚Äì4 outcomes as { id, label, votes }; options are always the source of counts.
- `votedId / defaultVotedId / onVote`: string | null / string / (id) => void ‚Äî Controlled or uncontrolled viewer vote.
- `allowRevote`: boolean (default false) ‚Äî Let the viewer move their vote between outcomes.
- `closesAt`: string | Date ‚Äî Compact mono CLOSES footer line.

Notes:
- Rows are aria-pressed buttons; each vote announces the new percentage and leadership once.
- Under reduced motion widths jump and the leader tick teleports; the readouts pulse instead of rolling.

### Balance Card (KB-115) ‚Äî `balance-card`
A balance with a private side.

A balance with a private side ‚Äî the numeral carry-rolls behind a privacy blur, a sparkline draws itself, and the card flips to its activity face.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/balance-card`

Props:
- `balance / format / delta / series`: number / (v)=>string / {value,direction} / number[] ‚Äî The carry-rolling amount, its delta chip, and the sparkline data.
- `activity`: BalanceActivity[] ‚Äî Back-face rows: { id, label, amount, time }.
- `onAction`: (action: "send" | "receive" | "convert") => void ‚Äî Fires from the action row.
- `defaultHidden`: boolean (default false) ‚Äî Start with the balance blurred.

Notes:
- The hidden face is inert and aria-hidden ‚Äî the flip never traps focus or leaks taps.
- Under reduced motion the flip is instant, the sparkline arrives drawn, and the blur toggles without a tween.

### Exchange Panel (KB-116) ‚Äî `exchange-panel`
Two units, one clean swap.

Two units, one clean swap ‚Äî the edited side is live, the computed side carry-rolls after a debounce, and swapping keeps focus while contents trade places.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/exchange-panel`

Props:
- `units`: ExchangeUnit[] | ExchangeGroup[] ‚Äî Linear factors to a shared base unit; groups render a grouped select.
- `feeRate`: number (default 0) ‚Äî Fraction applied to the converted side, shown as a fee row.
- `onChange / onDirectionChange`: (state) => void / (from, to) => void ‚Äî Conversion updates and swap events.
- `debounceMs`: number (default 200) ‚Äî Delay before the computed side rolls.

Notes:
- One side holds authority; the other is always derived ‚Äî swapping transfers authority with the gesture and refocuses the input.
- Under reduced motion contents swap instantly and the readout uses its highlight fallback.

### Utils ‚Äî `utils`
cn() ‚Äî class composition.

Class-name composition helper shared by every component.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/utils`

### Calibration Set ‚Äî `motion`
flick ¬∑ snap ¬∑ glide ¬∑ drift ¬∑ recoil.

Kinetiq's five calibrated springs plus the tween scale, cascade budget, and exit rules ‚Äî the shared physics vocabulary every component draws from.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/motion`

### useMotionSafe ‚Äî `use-motion-safe`
Reduced motion, decided once.

Single source of truth for reduced-motion decisions: combines the OS preference with an optional app-level override context.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/use-motion-safe`

### Spatial Set ‚Äî `spatial`
Perspective, detents, orbits, lift.

The shared geometry vocabulary of the spatial instruments ‚Äî house perspective range, angle detents, contact-lift shadows, orbit projection, and deterministic seeding.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/spatial`

### usePointerTilt ‚Äî `use-pointer-tilt`
Pointer ‚Üí sprung perspective.

Normalized pointer tracking mapped to sprung tilt values ‚Äî the house idiom for pointer-driven perspective, with an underdamped rebalance on leave and a fine-pointer gate.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/use-pointer-tilt`

### Agent Rules ‚Äî `agents-rules`
The design-system operating rules for agents.

Kinetiq's operating rules for coding agents: the five-spring motion language, the tween scale, the 600ms cascade budget, the reduced-motion policy, token usage, and composition guidance. Installs as AGENTS.md at your repo root so your agent stays on the system's vocabulary.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/agents-rules`
